questions.name: Required Field Completeness
questions.score_label: All 5 populated with non-trivial content
questions.evidence: model_details.name='Climate Forecasting Transformer v2.1'; overview describes transformer for regional climate prediction trained on 50 yrs NOAA data; licenses=[{identifier: 'Apache-2.0'}]; version.name='v2.1.0'; model_architecture='Custom Transformer with Spatiotemporal Attention: 12 encoder layers, 8 heads, hidden 512, 85M params'
questions.quality_note: All five required fields populated with concrete, model-specific content.

questions.name: Overview Length Adequacy
questions.score_label: >500 chars across overview/documentation with meaningful structure
questions.evidence: overview (~270 chars) + documentation (~260 chars) + short_description (~75 chars) combine to well over 500 chars; documentation describes spatiotemporal attention mechanism architecture.
questions.quality_note: Two-part narrative (overview + documentation) clearly exceeds 500 chars and provides meaningful structure.

questions.name: Tag / Keyword Diversity
questions.score_label: ≥4 tags AND pipeline_tag AND (language or model_category)
questions.evidence: tags=[climate, weather, forecasting, transformer, spatiotemporal, extreme-events, project:genesis, science:climate, risk:reviewed] (9 tags); pipeline_tag='climate-forecasting'; language=[en]
questions.quality_note: Nine domain-specific tags including namespaced project/science/risk tags, plus pipeline_tag and language.

questions.name: Input / Output Specification
questions.score_label: Input and output specified with shape/type; only input_format_map populated
questions.evidence: input_format describes 3D tensor [time_steps, lat_grid, lon_grid, variables] with explicit shapes; output_format describes probabilistic forecast tensor with quantiles; input_format_map has 5 entries (temperature, pressure, humidity, wind_u, wind_v); output_format_map not provided.
questions.quality_note: Excellent input documentation including shape and units; output is prose-described with shape but lacks an output_format_map structure.

questions.name: Schema Version Declared
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: schema_version: d4d-1.0
questions.quality_note: Recognizable schema version string declared at top of file.

questions.name: Persistent Identifier Present
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_details.references includes https://doi.org/10.1234/climate-ai-2025 (DOI) and GitHub repo URL; model_details.path='s3://climate-models/forecasting-transformer-v2.1/'
questions.quality_note: DOI is a true persistent identifier; S3 path and GitHub link supplement.

questions.name: Funding & Acknowledgements Completeness
questions.score_label: Funding agency + grant ID + acknowledgement of computing facility
questions.evidence: mission_relevance.funding_grants references DOE SciDAC Climate Modeling and NSF AI for Climate (grant references via D4D); mission_relevance.doe_project='GENESIS Climate AI Initiative'; doe_facility='OLCF Frontier, ALCF Polaris'; description acknowledges DOE Leadership Computing Facilities.
questions.quality_note: Two funding agencies (DOE, NSF) named with grant references and explicit acknowledgement of the OLCF Frontier and ALCF Polaris computing facilities.

questions.name: Ethical & Responsible-AI Documentation
questions.score_label: Ethical considerations + out_of_scope_uses populated; bias addressed within ethical_considerations rather than dedicated bias_* slots
questions.evidence: ethical_considerations: [Climate_justice with mitigation_strategy to evaluate across all regions including rural/underserved; Over_reliance_risk with mitigation to always communicate uncertainty]; out_of_scope_uses: 4 concrete items (long-term projections, regions outside NA, deterministic forecasts, emergency response without expert verification).
questions.quality_note: Strong ethical content with concrete mitigation strategies and detailed out_of_scope_uses; bias is addressed under ethical_considerations (climate justice) rather than explicit bias_model/output/input slots, slightly short of full criteria.

questions.name: License Clarity & SPDX Compliance
questions.score_label: SPDX identifier present; no explicit restriction text
questions.evidence: model_details.licenses: [{identifier: Apache-2.0}]
questions.quality_note: Clean SPDX identifier (Apache-2.0); no custom_text or restriction statement provided to clarify any boundaries beyond Apache-2.0 defaults.

questions.name: Framework / Library Standardization
questions.score_label: Framework + version pinned + library_name + Papers-with-Code-style model_index
questions.evidence: framework=PyTorch; framework_version=2.1.0; library_name=climate-forecasting; model_index entry with task, dataset, metrics, and source URL conforms to Papers-with-Code shape.
questions.quality_note: All four signals present with well-formed Papers-with-Code-style model_index entry.

questions.name: Tool & Software Transparency
questions.score_label: Comprehensive: framework_version + reproducibility-relevant libs + env pinning
questions.evidence: compute_infrastructure.software_dependencies contains a pinned pip-freeze (torch==2.1.0, transformers==4.35.0, numpy==1.24.3, xarray==2023.8.0, netCDF4==1.6.4, dask==2023.9.0); environment_config pins CUDA_VERSION=12.1, NCCL_VERSION=2.18, PYTORCH_CUDA_ALLOC_CONF; usage_documentation includes installation_instructions with conda env and pip install commands.
questions.quality_note: Excellent reproducibility documentation: pinned dependencies, CUDA/NCCL versions, conda env, install commands.

questions.name: Training Procedure Clarity
questions.score_label: Optimizer + LR + batch size + epochs/steps + schedule + regularization disclosed
questions.evidence: hyperparameters: optimizer=AdamW, learning_rate=0.0001, batch_size=32, training_epochs=50, training_steps=125000, optimization_techniques include mixed precision, gradient checkpointing, gradient clipping (max_norm=1.0), LR warmup (5000 steps), cosine annealing schedule; loss_function='Weighted MSE + quantile loss'; methodology describes DDP + fp16 + early stopping.
questions.quality_note: Complete set of hyperparameters and training methodology disclosed including curriculum-learning stages.

questions.name: Version History Documentation
questions.score_label: Semver name + ISO date + non-trivial diff
questions.evidence: version.name='v2.1.0' (semver); version.date='2025-01-15' (ISO); version.diff lists 3 concrete changes (15% extreme-weather detection improvement, added precipitation uncertainty quantification, 30% inference speedup via pruning); last_updated='2025-01-20T14:30:00Z'.
questions.quality_note: Clean semver, ISO date, and a substantive change log.

questions.name: Citations & References
questions.score_label: Citation with DOI AND multiple references with DOIs/URLs
questions.evidence: citations: APA-style entry for Smith et al. 2025 in Journal of Climate AI with DOI https://doi.org/10.1234/climate-ai-2025; references: GitHub repo URL and DOI URL.
questions.quality_note: Formal citation with DOI plus two distinct external references (code repo + DOI).

questions.name: Compute Infrastructure & Energy
questions.score_label: Hardware AND software listed; no GPU-hours/FLOPs or energy/carbon estimate
questions.evidence: compute_infrastructure.hardware='8x NVIDIA A100 80GB GPUs'; hardware_list includes OLCF Frontier MI250X and ALCF Polaris A100; software='PyTorch 2.1, CUDA 12.1'; training_speed='~2500 samples/sec on 8x A100'. No total GPU-hours, FLOPs, kWh, or carbon estimate disclosed.
questions.quality_note: Strong hardware/software disclosure including DOE LCFs, but no total compute budget or energy/carbon estimate to enable environmental accounting.

questions.name: Findability (Persistent Landing)
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: references include GitHub repo (https://github.com/climate-ai-lab/climate-transformer) and DOI (https://doi.org/10.1234/climate-ai-2025); model_details.path='s3://climate-models/forecasting-transformer-v2.1/'; model_index.source.url='https://benchmark.noaa.gov/climate-v3'.
questions.quality_note: Multiple resolvable landing URLs across code, publication, weights, and benchmark.

questions.name: Accessibility & Inference Path
questions.score_label: Runnable code example + I/O formats + library hooks
questions.evidence: usage_documentation includes installation_instructions, training_configuration, inference_configuration (full PyTorch snippet with .from_pretrained, .to('cuda'), torch.no_grad inference returning quantiles), and two complete code_examples (forecasting + extreme detection) in Python; matched by detailed input_format and output_format specs.
questions.quality_note: End-to-end runnable Python examples plus install/training/inference configurations and tensor I/O documentation.

questions.name: Performance Metrics with Slices & CI
questions.score_label: ≥2 metrics with numeric values AND ≥2 slices AND CIs present
questions.evidence: performance_metrics: RMSE_temperature=1.23 [CI 1.18-1.28, ±0.05] Kelvin slice='7-day forecast'; RMSE_precipitation=2.45 [CI 2.33-2.57, ±0.12] mm/day slice='7-day forecast'; F1_extreme_events=0.78 [CI 0.74-0.82] slice='Extreme heat/cold events'.
questions.quality_note: Three metrics each with numeric value, confidence interval, and slice; two metrics also include value_error.

questions.name: Out-of-Scope Uses, Limitations & Tradeoffs
questions.score_label: All three populated with concrete, model-specific content
questions.evidence: limitations: 4 items (7-day horizon, North America only, requires high-quality inputs, OOD extreme events); tradeoffs: 2 items (32x32 grid vs 3x compute; ensembles vs 10x inference cost); out_of_scope_uses: 4 items (long-term projections, non-NA regions, deterministic forecasts, emergency response without expert verification).
questions.quality_note: All three sections populated with concrete, model-specific content and explicit cost/benefit reasoning in tradeoffs.

questions.name: Cross-Platform Interlinks
questions.score_label: Pass
questions.evidence: model_index conforms to Papers-with-Code shape with task, dataset (NOAA Climate Prediction Benchmark v3), metrics, and source URL; model_parameters.training_datasets and evaluation_datasets reference D4D datasheet instances (noaa-historical-climate-dataset.yaml, era5-reanalysis, noaa-test-dataset.yaml, extreme-weather-benchmark); model_details.creator_references link to D4D Creator instances and an ORCID; mission_relevance.funding_grants reference D4D Grant instances; citations include DOI to publication.
questions.quality_note: Rich cross-platform interlinks across Papers-with-Code, D4D dataset/creator/grant instances, ORCID, and DOI publication.

strengths: Exceptional Performance & FAIRness section: full marks (17/17) with metric CIs, slices, comprehensive tradeoffs/limitations/out-of-scope content, and rich cross-platform interlinks (D4D datasets/creators/grants, Papers-with-Code model_index, ORCID, DOI).

Strong reproducibility: pinned framework version, full pip-freeze of dependencies, CUDA/NCCL versions, random_seed, environment_config, training pipeline_url, and complete hyperparameter disclosure (optimizer, LR, batch, epochs, steps, schedule, regularization).
Excellent funding and acknowledgements: two agencies (DOE, NSF) named via D4D grant references, plus explicit acknowledgement of OLCF Frontier and ALCF Polaris computing facilities.
Two runnable Python code examples (forecasting and extreme event detection) plus install/training/inference configurations make the inference path fully reproducible.
Diverse, well-namespaced tag set (9 tags including project:/science:/risk: namespaces) and Papers-with-Code-conformant model_index.
weaknesses: No total compute budget (GPU-hours/FLOPs) or energy/carbon estimate in compute_infrastructure, limiting environmental accounting (Q15: 3/5).
Bias is addressed via ethical_considerations (climate_justice) but no dedicated bias_model/bias_output/bias_input slots are populated (Q8: 4/5).
output_format_map is not populated even though input_format_map is (Q4: 4/5).
License is SPDX-clean (Apache-2.0) but no custom_text or restriction statement is provided (Q9: 4/5).
recommendations: Add total training compute (e.g., GPU-hours on Frontier/Polaris) and an energy or carbon estimate to compute_infrastructure to reach 5/5 on Q15.
Populate output_format_map with per-variable/quantile semantics to mirror input_format_map for full I/O coverage.
Add explicit bias_model / bias_output / bias_input slots (e.g., regional sampling bias toward NOAA-observed regions, calibration bias across precipitation magnitudes) to strengthen Q8.
Add a brief license custom_text statement clarifying redistribution and downstream-use boundaries to reach 5/5 on Q9.
